Project log for ADC project

Input files used

1st batch -> 39 chemo-naive HGSC patients from ONCOSYS-OVA.

TBA

2nd batch experiment -> ?? chemo-naive HGSC patients from ONCOSYS-OVA.

TBA

Results and analysis files

TBA

Developed code or analysis utilities

GeoMX:

Bulk RNA seq (deconvolutes, PDS (Ziqi)): S:\space_tcycif1\tCyCIF\6_bulk_rna\raw

Experiment plans

TBA

Computational & storage resources

Ethical approvals, how to report in papers, etc., in Oncosys-OVA samples & sample collection notebook

Clinical data (Sample data) in teams

Personel

Andreas Hainari, Anni Lindfors*, Ziqi Kang*, Ada Junquera, Nika Mikhailava, Iga Niemiec, Sara Palomino, Saundarya Shah, Anni Virtanen, Ulla-Maija Haltia, Anniina Färkkilä

*Shared second author (?)

Members of the project

Project leader: Andreas Hainari

Anni, Ulla-Majia,: Sample collection + clinical info

Ziqi: tCycif + help with analysis

Anni: Bulk RNA seq + GeoMX analysis

Sara, Ziqi, Ada, Nika, Iga: data preprocessing for the analysis

Saun: experiment (staining, validation experiment, scanning)

Andreas: tCycif + analysis, preprocessing, help and supervision of Anni

Project Log

12.5.2026, project meeting

We had a conversation about the upcoming project, and the slides can be found on GD. Next stops:

GANTT- timeline plot

Andreas, Ziqi and Anni will have an input on this - DONE

Anni’s thesis plan

Andreas will provide the initial plan/guidelines

tCycIF pictures will be ready within the next 1.5 months

Next meeting before Anniina’s holidays - Needs to be booked

18.5.2026, meeting with Anni, Iga, Sara and Ziqi

Meeting about the GeoMX and RNA analysis:

GeoMX

The areas are selected based on different immune cell areas

The immune cell fraction needs to be corrected always

This is also one limitation

Mostly PDS but also 10 (?) matched PDS-IDS samples

Based on staining done for tCycIF.

then divided for tumour and stroma

Use only tumour

58 samples!

Always check the ROIs since there might be 3 ROIs from around the same place

Check selection pictures and maybe exclude some

The data looks similar to a single-cell RNA matrix

Iga has preprocessed and deconvoluted everything

Ask Iga if something is unclear!!

One approach can be to include the target genes and then cluster based on these

So far, these results have been quite patient-specific

Bigger inter-patient differences than intra-tumoural

There is also metadata and the already normalised data

Iga has also made scripts and run gene expression analysis

Feel free to use!

There are preprocessing scripts in GitHub

can be run again, but no need, can help to understand the process though

Bulk RNA seq

Run with PRISM (or CYBERPRISM)

uses a single cell reference

different data sets for IDS and PDS

gives the results of what percentage of expression comes from which part of the sample

percentages and scaled expressions available

No way to validate for now, but correlated well with ASCAT purities (and tumour compartment)

Trustworthy for big cell types

22.5.2026, thesis meeting with Anni

-jos päällekköin ei merkittävä

-metrics interpatient

Progress from Anni: Initial bulk RNA analyses: current script contains the following

batch correction: ComBat (to be confirmed with Sara if this is OK)

ADC target specificity to tumor: DE analysis (limma, paired design: tumor vs non-tumor), and violin plot of expressions between cell compartments

Variability/correlation between ADC targets: spearman correlation and boxplot

co-expression: genome-wide ADC coexpression heatmap; which genes are co-expressed with ADC targets?

extreme group DE analysis: which genes are DE between high and low ADC expressors

continuing with: add vtcn1 to ADC target list, include info from new patient metadata dataframe when finished, include IDS data when exported (possible comparisons between PDS and IDS samples), start GeoMx data analyses, read articles

To have more power for tumour vs non-tumour, we would need to export the rest of the non-tumour sample data. Do we want to continue with this approach or not? The decision will be made later.

For the analysis, if the expression is high in weird genes that are overlapping in segmental data, we need to be careful when saying it would be of any significance

The result would be a nice metric on heterogeneity interpatiently, which can be compared to intra-tumourous expression.

This metric can be defined better from GeoMX data.

Anni adds data modalities to the script that’ll query all of the data we have
